title: croton decalvatus
intro: Croton decalvatus, also known as the white croton, is a plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family native to the tropical regions of Africa and Madagascar. The medicinal properties of Croton decalvatus lie in its leaves, which contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, Croton decalvatus is used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, and its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to make teas and ointments.
